pages.title: Orchestrator Portal
pages.description: Welcome To The Orchestrator Portal: Explore, Earn, Operate

pages.title: Find Your Path to Becoming a Livepeer Orchestrator
pages.description: Not sure where to start? Use this guide to find the best path for your goals and experience level.

pages.title: Orchestrator Role & Overview
pages.description: Learn what orchestrators are, how they earn, and which setup path fits your GPU resources and goals.

pages.title: Orchestrator Capabilities and Functions
pages.description: Learn what orchestrators do across compute, AI inference, staking, delegation, and governance on the Livepeer network.

pages.title: Job Types
pages.description: The job types an orchestrator can process — transcoding, batch AI inference, real-time AI (Cascade), and LLM inference — with hardware requirements, pricing models, and how to choose which to run.

pages.title: Orchestrator Architecture
pages.description: See how gateways, orchestrator controllers, and GPU workers interact to process transcoding and AI jobs.

pages.title: Orchestrator Participation Model
pages.description: Understand orchestrator revenue, costs, staking, fees, and incentives across Livepeer network participation.

pages.title: How to Get Started
pages.description: Choose your path as a GPU operator on Livepeer — join a pool, run a solo orchestrator, add AI pipelines, or operate at enterprise scale.

pages.description: Run go-livepeer as a transcoding orchestrator: create your wallet, configure your node, register, and verify.

pages.description: Get your orchestrator up and running with AI orchestration capabilities.

pages.title: Install go-livepeer
pages.description: Install the go-livepeer binary on Linux, macOS, or via Docker. Covers prerequisites, GPU driver requirements, installation steps, and verification.

pages.description: Set the go-livepeer flags needed to run as an orchestrator — GPU selection, pricing, networking, and AI worker configuration.

pages.description: Connect go-livepeer to the Arbitrum network using a hosted or self-hosted RPC endpoint — required for staking, activation, and payments.

pages.title: AI Pipelines
pages.description: Configure and optimise AI inference pipelines on your orchestrator — pipeline types, aiModels.json reference, custom models, Ollama LLM runner, and remote workers.
